question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user inject the environment variable HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DOWNLOADER_RECORD_ENABLED=true into a misconfigured deployment to force the record downloader to remain active and process unsigned or minimally-signed transaction records, bypassing any operator-level disable intended to halt ingestion during a key compromise, causing unauthorized transactions to be persisted to the mirror database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the publicly-exposed Lombok-generated setEnabled() setter (via @Data) through a Spring Boot Actuator /actuator/env endpoint left open without authentication to flip enabled from false to true at runtime, causing the RecordFileDownloader to resume ingesting transaction records that were intentionally halted due to missing signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.frequency to an extremely small Duration (e.g., 1ms) via an externally-writable config source, causing the @Scheduled download() loop to fire at near-zero intervals and overwhelm the signature verification pipeline, leading to race conditions where transaction records are accepted before all required node signatures are collected and validated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: setFrequency(Duration)] Can an unprivileged external user set frequency to a very large Duration value (e.g., Duration.ofDays(365)) via environment variable injection, causing the @Scheduled(fixedDelayString) in RecordFileDownloader.download() to effectively halt record ingestion, allowing an attacker-controlled node to inject forged transaction records into the cloud bucket during the gap, which are then accepted when the downloader eventually resumes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user set persistBytes=true via environment variable injection so that raw record file bytes are stored in the database, and then exploit a separate read path to extract the raw bytes of transaction records including those with sensitive signer data, enabling offline forgery of future transactions that appear to have valid owner signatures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: setPersistBytes(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user toggle persistBytes from false to true via the Lombok-generated setter exposed through an unauthenticated Spring Boot Actuator endpoint, causing streamFile.setBytes(null) to be skipped in Downloader.verifySignatures(), so that raw transaction bytes (including unapproved or partially-signed transactions) are retained in memory and eventually persisted to the database without proper owner authorization checks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user set writeFiles=true via environment variable injection, causing Utility.archiveFile() to write verified record stream files to the local filesystem, and then exploit a path traversal vulnerability in the archive destination path to overwrite critical configuration files that control signer key validation, effectively authorizing future transactions without legitimate owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: setWriteFiles(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user enable writeFiles via an externally-accessible configuration endpoint, causing the RecordFileDownloader.downloadSidecars() method to also write sidecar files (which contain contract state changes and bytecode) to disk, leaking sensitive transaction execution data that reveals signer patterns and owner account structures, enabling targeted forgery of transactions that bypass approval checks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user set writeSignatures=false (the default) to ensure that no signature files are archived to disk, eliminating the audit trail of which nodes signed which transaction records, so that if a malicious record file is later accepted due to a consensus manipulation attack, there is no forensic evidence of the missing or forged signer approvals?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: setWriteSignatures(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user set writeSignatures=true via environment variable injection, causing all signature files (including those from nodes with compromised keys) to be written to the archive directory, and then read those files to extract node public key usage patterns, enabling a targeted attack to forge the minimum number of signatures required to meet the consensusRatio threshold and authorize transactions without legitimate owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getStreamType() hardcodes StreamType.RECORD (which has isChained()=true) to craft a record file with a manipulated previousHash field that matches the last accepted file's hash, bypassing the hash chain verification in Downloader.verifyHashChain() and causing a forged transaction record to be accepted as if it were legitimately signed by the required node owners?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user inject hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.consensusRatio=0 via environment variable, causing ConsensusValidatorImpl.validate() to skip the consensus check entirely (the BigDecimal.ZERO.equals() branch), so that a single node's signature (or even no valid signatures) is sufficient to authorize and ingest transaction records without approval from the required fraction of node owners?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.downloadRatio to the minimum allowed value (equal to consensusRatio) via environment variable injection, causing partialCollection() in Downloader to query only the minimum number of nodes needed for consensus, so that an attacker who controls just those few nodes can supply forged signatures and authorize transactions without approval from the majority of legitimate signers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RecordFileDownloader.shouldDownload() always returns true (overriding the base class check of downloaderProperties.isEnabled()) to ensure that even when enabled=false is set in RecordDownloaderProperties, the downloadNextBatch() method still executes if called directly, allowing transaction records to be processed without the enabled guard that operators rely on to halt ingestion during signer key rotation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationProperties binding of hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.enabled to inject a crafted Spring Cloud Config response (if the importer uses a remote config server) that sets enabled=true, overriding a local disable and causing the downloader to process transaction records from a malicious cloud bucket that contains records signed by attacker-controlled nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user set frequency to a Duration that evaluates to 0 milliseconds (e.g., PT0S) via environment variable, causing the @Scheduled(fixedDelayString) annotation to schedule the download() method with zero delay, creating a tight loop that processes transaction records faster than the signature verification pipeline can validate node approvals, leading to race conditions where unverified transactions are accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.batchSize to a very large value via environment variable injection, causing downloadAndParseSigFiles() to download an enormous number of signature files in a single batch, overwhelming the NodeSignatureVerifier and causing it to skip or incorrectly process some signatures, resulting in transaction records being accepted without the full required set of signer approvals?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.maxSize to a very large value (bypassing the @Min(2L) constraint via direct property injection), causing the downloader to accept oversized record files that contain malformed or padded transaction data designed to confuse the hash verification logic, allowing transactions without proper owner signatures to pass the file hash check?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.allowAnonymousAccess=true via environment variable injection, causing the downloader to use anonymous credentials when accessing the cloud storage bucket, and then perform a bucket substitution attack by pointing the downloader at an attacker-controlled bucket containing record files with forged transactions that appear to have valid node signatures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.endpointOverride to an attacker-controlled S3-compatible server via environment variable injection, causing the downloader to fetch record files and signature files from the malicious endpoint, where the attacker serves crafted files with forged node signatures that meet the consensusRatio threshold, authorizing transactions without legitimate signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.bucketName to an attacker-controlled cloud bucket via environment variable injection, causing RecordDownloaderProperties.getCommon().getBucketName() to return the malicious bucket name, and then serve crafted record files from that bucket containing transactions authorized by attacker-controlled node keys that meet the minimum consensusRatio?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.pathType=NODE_ID via environment variable injection, causing the downloader to use a different bucket path structure (HIP-679 node ID based) that the attacker has pre-populated with forged record files and signature files, bypassing the expected ACCOUNT_ID path structure and causing the downloader to accept transactions without proper owner authorization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.pathPrefix to a crafted prefix string via environment variable injection, causing the downloader to read record files from an attacker-controlled subdirectory within the legitimate bucket, where the attacker has placed forged transaction records with fabricated node signatures that satisfy the consensusRatio check?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.timeout to an extremely small Duration (e.g., 1ms) via environment variable injection, causing all legitimate node signature downloads to time out in Downloader.downloadAndParseSigFiles(), resulting in an empty signature set that triggers the DEMO network disable path or causes the downloader to skip signature verification entirely for that batch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.sources to an attacker-controlled list of stream sources via environment variable injection, causing the downloader to fetch record files from malicious endpoints that serve transaction records signed only by attacker-controlled nodes, bypassing the requirement for legitimate signer approval from the actual Hedera network nodes?
